title: Custom Database Connectors
text: Custom Database Connectors Uniface provides DBMS connectors for many industry-recognized relational database systems, but you can also build your own. The use of connectors to support I/O interaction with DBMSs enables a Uniface application to be independent of the databases in use. Database connectors are software modules that implement a well-defined set of functions for a particular DBMS. They translate Uniface I/O requests into the Data Definition Language (DDL) or Data Manipulation Language (DML) of a DBMS using the database's API. The DBMS connector interface of the Uniface runtime system uses connector function calls to communicate with all the DBMS connectors. The connector itself communicates with the DBMS through the Application Programming Interface (API) of the DBMS. Figure 1. DBMS Connector in the Uniface Architecture The library functions provided by the API define how to exchange data between the DBMS and the DBMS connector. If a query language is supported, the API defines the syntax and semantics of the language. Note: Ensure you are very familiar with the API of your DBMS before attempting to write a DBMS connector. If you want to use a transaction manager to control a database connector, you will need to build a TM-capable database connector. Uniface does not provide TM-controlled connectors and does not directly support third-party transaction managers. For more information, see TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors . Was this topic helpful?
